narrative:
Shoja' Khalilzadeh is a Centre-Back for Iran, whose overall rating of 7.19/10 places him among the top 4% of centre-backs at this tournament. This significant rating reflects both his consistent club form over the last two seasons and his contributions in the current World Cup. He has played 270 minutes in the tournament, establishing himself as a key defensive presence.

Khalilzadeh exhibits strong defensive capabilities, averaging 2.0 tackles and 1.0 interception per 90 minutes at this World Cup. His effectiveness in clearing danger is evident with 9.333 clearances per 90 minutes. Offensively, his expected goals per 90 minutes stands at a low 0.049, underscoring his primary role as a defensive specialist.

As a seasoned player, Khalilzadeh anchors Iran's defence, with 66% of his overall rating derived from his club form, suggesting consistent quality. Scouts and fans should watch for his continued defensive solidity and ability to organize the backline in their remaining matches. His high volume of clearances will be critical in nullifying opposition attacks.
